subject: accounting
text: A set of rules and procedures guiding the operations of a partnership is called partnership
explanation: Partnership deed is a document drawn up by partners, which contains the rules and regulations guiding the business. It is also a document, which will clarify the respective positions and duties of the partners in a business.

subject: accounting
text: Which of the following rules is applicable in the absence of a partnership agreement? Interest is payable

options.A: on drawings at the rate of 5% per annum
options.B: on any contribution in excess of agreed capital at 5% per annum
options.C: at 5% per annum on the current account balances
options.D: on agreed capital contributed at 5% per annum

explanation: If no specific agreement is made by the partners, 5% interest a year on loans made by partners in excess of the agreed capitals.

subject: accounting
text: Shares which are issued at no cost to existing shareholders in proportion to existing shares held are
explanation: Bonus issue of shares occurred when shares are issued to existing share holders for free. This is a way of pacifying them not receiving dividend.

subject: accounting
text: A collection of fields relating to one logically definable unit of business information is known as
explanation: Database - is an integrated collection of logically related records or files. A database consolidates records previously stored in separate files into a common pool of data records that provides data for many applications.

subject: accounting
text: Debts that a firm is unable to recover are debited to bad debts account and credited to
explanation: The entry to write off a bad debt account affects only balance sheet accounts: a debit to Allowance for Doubtful Accounts and a credit to Accounts Receivable. Accounts receivable is money owed to a company by its debtors.

subject: accounting
text: Subscription in advance is treated in the balance sheet under
explanation: Subscription in advance is the sum of money prepaid by the members of club representing their dues for next year. It is treated as a liability in the balance sheet.

subject: accounting
text: The two column cash book records

options.A: all receipts in cash only and payments by cheque
options.B: receipts and payments in cash only
options.C: all receipts and payments both in cash and by cheque
options.D: receipts and payments by cheque only

explanation: The two column cash book is a combination of cash account and bank account in one book. It shows the total particulars of all the money received and paid by the firm either in cash or cheque.

subject: accounting
text: Which of the following items will be recorded on the credit side of a club's income and expenditure account?
explanation: Donations received is an income to the organization. All incomes appear on the credit side of income and expenditure account.

subject: accounting
text: The principle of double entry bookkeeping states that

options.A: every debtor must have a creditor
options.B: every account debited must be immediately credited
options.C: for every debit entry, there must be a corresponding credit entry
options.D: for every double debit, there must be a double credit

explanation: The double entry principle of bookkeeping states that, for every credit entry, there must be a corresponding debit entry and vice versa

subject: accounting
text: When a fixed asset is disposed off, the accounting entries to write off the asset are Debit

options.A: Profit and loss account; credit asset disposal account
options.B: Asset disposal account; credit profit and loss account
options.C: Asset disposal account, credit asset account
options.D: Profit and loss account; credit provision for depreciation account

explanation: How to record the disposal of assets No proceeds, fully depreciated. Debit all accumulated depreciation and credit the fixed asset . Loss on sale. Debit cash for the amount received, debit all accumulated depreciation, debit the loss on sale of asset account, and credit the fixed asset . Gain on sale.

explanation: Overhead costs, also known as indirect costs, are expenses that cannot be directly attributed to a specific product or service. explanation: The sales ledger control account is also called total debtors control account. It is the control account for sales or debtors ledger.

subject: accounting
text: A reason a business is not able to keep full set of accounting records is that

options.A: the business does not make profit
options.B: double entry is expensive to maintain
options.C: the business is not a trading concern
options.D: double entry shows the true profit

explanation: The double entry system is comparatively an expensive way of maintaining the financial accounts. The accountants may charge a handsome amount as fees. Maintaining incomplete records consumes less time.

subject: accounting
text: The debit side of a trial balance total is GH¢ 2,000 more than the credit side. Which of the following errors would account for the difference?

options.A: A receipt of GH¢ 2,000 for rent has been omitted from the books
options.B: Carriage inwards of GH¢ 2,000 has been debited to carriage outwards account
options.C: Petty cash balance of GH¢ 1,000 has been omitted from the books
options.D: Discount received of GH¢ 1,000 has been debited to discount allowed account

explanation: GH¢ 2,000 received from rent is an income that should have appeared in the credit side of the trial balance. This error of omission would definitely make the debit side to be GH¢ 2,000 higher than the credit side.
